2603.25640.title: RenoBench: A Citation Parsing Benchmark
2603.25640.paper_summary: Accurate parsing of citations is necessary for machine-readable scholarly infrastructure. But, despite sustained interest in this problem, existing evaluation techniques are often not generalizable, based on synthetic data, or not publicly available. We introduce RenoBench, a public domain benchmark for citation parsing, sourced from PDFs released on four publishing ecosystems: SciELO, Redalyc, the Public Knowledge Project, and Open Research Europe. Starting from 161,000 annotated citations, we apply automated validation and feature-based sampling to produce a dataset of 10,000 citations spanning multiple languages, publication types, and platforms. We then evaluate a variety of citation parsing systems and report field-level precision and recall. Our results show strong performance from language models, particularly when fine-tuned. RenoBench enables reproducible, standardized evaluation of citation parsing systems, and provides a foundation for advancing automated citation parsing and metascientific research.
2603.25640.paper_summary_zh: 準確解析引用對於機器可讀的學術基礎設施是必要的。<lb/>但是，儘管對這個問題持續有興趣，現有的評估技術往往不具可推廣性，基於合成數據，或未公開可用。<lb/>我們介紹了RenoBench，一個公共領域的引用解析基準，來源於在四個出版生態系統上發布的PDF：SciELO、Redalyc、公共知識計劃和開放研究歐洲。<lb/>從161,000個標註的引用開始，我們應用自動驗證和基於特徵的抽樣來生成一個包含10,000個引用的數據集，涵蓋多種語言、出版類型和平台。<lb/>然後，我們評估各種引用解析系統並報告領域級的精確度和召回率。<lb/>我們的結果顯示語言模型表現強勁，特別是在微調後。<lb/>RenoBench使引用解析系統的可重複、標準化評估成為可能，並為推進自動化引用解析和元科學研究提供了基礎。
2603.25640.author: Parth Sarin et.al.
2603.25640.authors: Parth Sarin, Juan Pablo Alperin, Adam Buttrick, Dione Mentis
